authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.50(b)(92) — McLeod Lake (Mono Co.)
sources.quotedText: (92) McLeod Lake (Mono Co.). All year. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: 2026 Provisional Stocking Document for Trout and Inland Salmon — CDFW
sources.quotedText: R6 Mono McCleod Lake Lahontan Cutthroat Trout Fingerling Put and Grow August-September

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Lake layer record for gaz_id 274662, "McLeod Lake", Lake, Mono County, CA
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":274662,"gaz_name":"McLeod Lake","gaz_featureclass":"Lake","fcode":39004,"state_alpha":"CA","county_name":"Mono"}; geometry (Web Mercator -13250426.1638, 4524209.0471) converts to lon -119.030603, lat 37.608090, matching the file's recorded coordinate (-119.0308, 37.6080) to within ~10m.

parameters.note: McCleod Lake (spelled 'McLeod Lake' in the regulations, GNIS, and OpenStreetMap) is listed in CCR §7.50(b)(92) as a special-regulation water: open all year, artificial lures with barbless hooks only, zero-trout bag limit.

regulations.humanSummary: Open all year, catch-and-release only (artificial lures with barbless hooks; zero-trout bag limit)

parameters.note: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used.

regulations.humanSummary: Artificial lures with barbless hooks only
regulations.verbatimText: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used.

parameters.counting_rule: 0 trout — all trout caught must be released; catch-and-release only.

regulations.humanSummary: Zero trout bag limit — catch-and-release only

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

stockingSchedule.note: CDFW's 2026 Provisional Stocking Document for Trout and Inland Salmon lists McCleod Lake (Region 6, Mono Co.) for fingerling-size Lahontan Cutthroat Trout under the Put and Grow program, with a stated release month range of August-September. This is CDFW's provisional plan for 2026, not a guaranteed annual constant.
